text:
Artistic swimming has long-been considered one of the more unconventional events at the summerOlympics, but the sport could gain a new legion of fans after Team USA’s spectacular performance.

A combination of water aerobics set to music may not be for everyone, but there can be no doubting the levels of co-ordination and physical fitness required to compete at an elite level.

USA are participating in both the duet and team competitions for the first time since Beijing in 2008 in a sport that used to be known as synchronised swimming.

The sport was rebranded eight years ago, after the 2016 Rio Olympics, when the international governing body, World Aquatics, changed the name with a view to increasing visibility and better showcasing the essence of the sport.

Regardless of whether you wish to the call the sport by it’s new moniker, or it’s more traditional predecessor, you would be hard pushed not to have been impressed by USA’s routine which included an incredible underwater moonwalk, while Michael Jackson’s Smooth Criminal blaring away in the background.

The judges, however, appeared nonplussed with the performance, awarding USA 249.0067 points which sees them sit in a lowly seventh place after the opening round.

Chinatook the top position after delivering a fast-paced technical routine that was scored well aboveSpain, who sit in the silver-medal position.

The Chinese team proved they will be the country to beat as they chase their first gold in the sport.Russiahave dominated since 2000, but their exclusion from the Paris Games following the invasion of Ukraine means that China and other countries finally have an opening to the top of the podium.

Team USA moonwalked upside down, underwater ð¤¯. Iâll never understand how they train for this.pic.twitter.com/DAu0DFVEvM

China’s team impressed the crowd before the group even entered the water, as one swimmer stood atop her seven teammates before launching herself into the pool.

Wearing red-and-black sequined swimsuits, the women performed to a composition called “Speed and Passion” by Wu Mingxuan, and the routine lived up to the name of the music.

The team tossed one swimmer into the air who performed several twists before landing in the water, drawing excitement from the spectators.

Judges awarded the routine 313.55 points, 26 points higher than Spain in second place.

Italy, who gave an energetic performance with a disco dance theme, sit in the bronze-medal position.

The competition for the gold medal in team artistic swimming will be determined by three performances — the technical, free and acrobatic routines — the last of which is being introduced for the first time in these Games.

title: Team USA perform incredible underwater Olympic dance routine to iconic 80s pop anthem | Metro News
description: Team USA sit in seventh place following the Team Technical Routine at the Paris Olympics as they search for gold in the artistic swimming
